channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ASX set to open lower amid Wall Street tech losses and AI uncertainty
item.description: Australian shares are predicted to open lower, mirroring losses on Wall Street as renewed concerns about AI investments ripple through markets. Tech stocks led the retreat in the US, with the S&P 500 ...

item.title: SA Government Extends Fuel Levy Relief to Support Motorists Amid Rising Prices

item.title: US Intelligence Reviews Iran’s Possible Response to Trump Ending Military Operations
item.description: US intelligence agencies are closely studying the potential reactions from Iran if President Donald Trump were to declare victory in the ongoing conflict, now spanning two months, which has already cl...

item.title: West Bengal adds 1,468 voters to electoral rolls ahead of second polling phase
item.description: Ahead of the second phase of the West Bengal Assembly elections, 1,468 names have been reinstated in the electoral rolls by the appellate tribunals under the special intensive revision process. This u...

item.title: Assessment of Canada’s Fiscal Update and Economic Challenges
item.description: Andrew Coyne critiques the recent Spring Economic Update from the Carney government, arguing that it offers little meaningful change to Canada's persistent economic challenges. The update fails to add...

item.title: Lance Reddick: Career Highlights and Net Worth at the Time of His Passing
item.description: Lance Reddick, an acclaimed American actor known for his roles in 'The Wire' and the 'John Wick' series, had an estimated net worth of $3 to $4 million at the time of his death on March 17, 2023. Born...

item.title: Woman Climbs Mobile Tower in Attempt to Reunite With Her Lover
item.description: In a rather unusual incident in India, a married woman reportedly climbed a mobile phone tower, stating that her intention was to be with her lover. According to police reports, she took this drastic ...

item.title: Humpback whale 'Timmy' safely moved to rescue barge off Germany
item.description: On Tuesday afternoon, relief and excitement filled the air as rescue teams successfully guided a stranded humpback whale, named Timmy, into a floating tank on a barge off Germany's Baltic coast. The o...

item.title: Man Admits to Planning Attack on Taylor Swift Concert in Austria
item.description: A man has pleaded guilty for plotting an attack on a Taylor Swift concert held in Vienna, Austria. The suspect, whose name was not disclosed, admitted his intentions to authorities, raising concerns o...

item.title: Melania Trump says she is 'very well' after security scare at White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: First Lady Melania Trump has spoken publicly for the first time since a security incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner on Saturday 25 April 2026. When asked by reporters how she was copin...

item.title: Assessing the Humanitarian and Socio-Economic Effects of the Middle East Conflict on Women and Girls
item.description: Six weeks into the 2026 escalation of conflict in the Middle East, the UN Population Fund (UNFPA) reports that the effects on 161 million women and girls living in affected areas remain largely unaddr...

item.title: Scholar transport operators in Eastern Cape to march over unpaid services
item.description: In the Eastern Cape, scholar transport operators are set to stage a peaceful protest on Wednesday due to nonpayment by the provincial government. The demonstration, confirmed by the South African Nati...

item.description: The 2026 West Bengal elections are shaping up to be influenced by a variety of socio-political factors. While food references like 'fish' and 'jhalmuri' have entered the discussion, they symbolically ...

item.title: Jackson Irvine criticises FIFA for awarding Peace Prize to Trump amid human rights concerns

item.title: Government Commences Construction of Kabarnet University in Baringo
item.description: The Kenyan government has officially kicked off the construction of Kabarnet University, the first public university in Baringo County, with a Sh1 billion budget. The groundbreaking ceremony, held at ...
